Title: Integrating Carbon-Halogen Bond Formation into Medicinal Plant Metabolism

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables downstream incorporation of 5-chlorotryptamine into chlorinated alkaloids; without STRvm, 5-chlorotryptophan and 5-chlorotryptamine accumulate but no downstream chlorinated alkaloids are detected.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in transgenic hairy roots
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hairy root transformation; LC-MS metabolite profiling; comparison of lines with and without STRvm
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR for transgene transcript levels; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enabling module for engineering 5-halogenated monoterpene indole alkaloids in planta when native strictosidine synthase rejects halogenated substrate.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Native pathway blockage at strictosidine synthase prevents use of 5-chlorotryptamine. 2. Mutant STRvm bypasses this block and allows formation of 10-chloroajmalicine and 15-chlorotabersonine. 3. In the absence of STRvm, upstream halogenated intermediates accumulate but no downstream alkaloids are produced.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Engineered mutant introduced because native strictosidine synthase cannot turn over 5-chlorotryptamine.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: engineered halogenation entry into monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 7-halotryptophan/7-halotryptamine route to chlorinated or brominated monoterpene indole alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Regioselectively halogenates the indole ring of tryptophan at the 7 position using halide and reduced flavin supplied by RebF.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms precursor pool for downstream chlorinated/brominated alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: medicinal alkaloid analogs with potentially altered pharmacological properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression with RebF in C. roseus generates 7-chlorotryptophan and downstream chlorinated alkaloids; with bromide supplementation also yields brominated alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in transgenic hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: codon-optimized transgenic expression in hairy roots; LC-MS metabolite profiling; co-elution with authentic standards; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: low-chloride medium plus KBr supports brominated product formation; KI supplementation does not yield iodinated products
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: halogen-producing lines were thicker and slower growing than wild type
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR for transgene transcript levels; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates that bacterial halogenases can be inserted into plant specialized metabolism to generate new medicinal alkaloid analogs with predictable regioselectivity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RebH functioned productively in plant cells when co-expressed with RebF. 2. It generated 7-chlorotryptophan that was incorporated into chlorinated alkaloids, especially 12-chloro-19,20-dihydroakuammicine. 3. Under bromide supplementation, RebH also enabled brominated alkaloid production, whereas iodination was not observed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No signal peptide was added so product formation occurred in the cytosol; product profile suggests downstream enzyme specificity for 7-chlorotryptamine-derived intermediates.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: engineered halogenation entry into monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 5-halotryptophan/5-halotryptamine route to chlorinated monoterpene indole alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Regioselectively halogenates tryptophan at the 5 position using reduced flavin provided by RebF.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms precursor pool for downstream chlorinated alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: medicinal alkaloid analogs with potentially altered pharmacological properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression with RebF and STRvm in C. roseus generates 5-chlorotryptophan and multiple downstream chlorinated alkaloids; without STRvm only upstream halogenated intermediates accumulate.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in transgenic hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: codon-optimized transgenic expression in hairy roots; LC-MS metabolite profiling; co-elution with authentic standards; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: halogen-producing lines were thicker and slower growing than wild type
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR for transgene transcript levels; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides a route for engineering 5-chlorinated alkaloid analogs, but requires co-engineering of downstream pathway enzymes with broader substrate tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PyrH functioned in plant cells to generate 5-chlorotryptophan. 2. Production of downstream chlorinated alkaloids required co-expression of STRvm because the native strictosidine synthase does not accept 5-chlorotryptamine. 3. Representative products included 10-chloroajmalicine and 15-chlorotabersonine.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Revealed a pathway compatibility constraint at strictosidine synthase for the 5-chloro branch.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: engineered halogenation support module for monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: reduced flavin supply for RebH/PyrH-mediated tryptophan halogenation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Supplies reduced flavin required for halogenase activity of RebH and PyrH.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies cofactor for precursor-forming halogenation step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: chlorinated and brominated monoterpene indole alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: medicinal alkaloid analogs with potentially altered pharmacological properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression of RebF together with RebH or PyrH was required for production of halogenated compounds in C. roseus.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in transgenic hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression requirement in transgenic hairy roots; LC-MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: standard or low-chloride/bromide-supplemented media depending experiment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: halogen-producing lines were thicker and slower growing than wild type
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR for transgene transcript levels; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential support gene for transferring flavin-dependent halogenation modules into plants.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RebF was necessary for productive halogenase activity in planta. 2. Halogenated compounds were only observed when both RebF and the cognate halogenase were expressed. 3. RebF enabled both chlorination and bromination through the engineered pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as required redox partner rather than direct scaffold-modifying enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: entry step from tryptophan to tryptamine pool
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: in vivo bottleneck for halogenated precursor conversion
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates tryptophan and halogenated tryptophans to the corresponding tryptamines.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for strictosidine and downstream monoterpene indole alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: medicinal alkaloid analogs with potentially altered pharmacological properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Accepts chlorinated tryptophan substrates in vitro and in vivo, enabling halogenated alkaloid biosynthesis, but with much lower catalytic efficiency than for native tryptophan, creating a bottleneck.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme kinetics with halogenated substrates; metabolite accumulation patterns in transgenic roots
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: accumulation of chlorotryptophans indicates precursor processing bottleneck; may divert halogenated intermediates to other pathways such as auxin metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering improved decarboxylase activity toward halogenated tryptophans could raise yields of halogenated alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C. roseus tryptophan decarboxylase decarboxylated both 5- and 7-chlorotryptophan in vitro. 2. Its catalytic efficiency for halogenated substrates was about 30-fold lower than for tryptophan. 3. Accumulation of chlorotryptophans in transgenic lines indicated that this step is a bottleneck in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although endogenous rather than heterologously introduced, it was experimentally interrogated as a core determinant of pathway compatibility with halogenated precursors.
